Features How it works Homebrew Download

Your Claude Code skills,
finally in one place.

A tiny Mac app to install, browse, and copy every command you own. Drag a skill onto it — a SKILL.md, a folder, or a .zip — and it lands exactly where Claude Code looks for it.

Why you'll like it

Small app, big quality-of-life

No accounts, no cloud, no setup. It runs locally and works offline.

📥

Drag-and-drop install

Drop a skill file, folder, or zip. It figures out the name, fixes the header if needed, and puts it in ~/.claude/skills/.

🔎

Search everything

Find any command by what it does. Hit / and type — results filter instantly.

📋

One-click copy

Every card has a copy button. Paste the /command straight into Claude Code.

🧩

Live skill list

See exactly what's installed, with a badge for which ones are valid and ready to run.

🔌

Offline & private

A tiny local helper — nothing leaves your Mac. Open it like any app and go.

🛡️

Signed & notarized

Notarized by Apple, so it opens with a normal double-click. No scary warnings.

Dead simple

From download to done in a minute

1

Install the app

Download the DMG and drag Command Center to your Applications folder (or use Homebrew).

2

Drop in your skills

Drag any skill onto the window. It installs into ~/.claude/skills/ automatically.

3

Browse & copy

Search your library, copy a /command, and paste it into Claude Code. That's it.

For the terminal crowd

Prefer Homebrew?

Two lines and you're done. Upgrades come through brew too.

🍺 Terminal
$brew tap mrenee1/command-center
$brew install --cask command-center

First time only, Homebrew asks you to trust the tap: brew trust mrenee1/command-center

Ready when you are

Download Command Center

Universal build for Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.

⬇ Download .dmg · v1.0.1 · 343 KB
✓ Apple-notarizedSHA-256 eb2b1958ddf4…
Is it safe? Why does my Mac trust it?

The app is code-signed with an Apple Developer ID and notarized by Apple, so Gatekeeper opens it with a normal double-click — no “unidentified developer” warning. The download is checksum-verified (SHA-256 eb2b1958ddf4…).

What does it need to run?

macOS 11 or later and Python 3 (already on most Macs). If Python 3 is missing, the app points you to a one-line install. Nothing else — no accounts, no internet.

Where do my skills go?

Straight into ~/.claude/skills/, the folder Claude Code reads on startup. Restart Claude Code (or open a new chat) after installing a skill and it shows up as /your-skill.

Does anything get uploaded?

No. Command Center runs a small local helper on your own machine. Your files never leave your Mac.

Download Homebrew GitHub
